items.title: [Boing Boing] This air quality monitor is a cute bird that dies when you need to air out your home
items.content_html:
Birdie has a simple tag line: "Dead bird: open your window." Harsh, but clear. Like the coal mine canaries of yore, Birdie collapses when air quality drops and revives when it improves.

In Germany, Lüften, regularly opening windows for short periods, regardless of the season or outside temperature, is common, as it improves indoor air quality and sleep, and helps prevent mold. — Read the rest

The post This air quality monitor is a cute bird that dies when you need to air out your home appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [Boing Boing] Your WiFi router knows it's you
items.content_html:
Turns out you may not need to carry a tracking device everywhere if the room itself is already doing the job: researchers say ordinary WiFi can recognize individuals with startling accuracy just from the way their bodies disturb radio waves.

"By observing the propagation of radio waves, we can create an image of the surroundings and of persons who are present," says Professor Thorsten Strufe from KASTEL — KIT's Institute of Information Security and Dependability.

— Read the rest

The post Your WiFi router knows it's you appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.content_html: At CES 2026, MSI unveiled a new lineup of power supplies under its MPG and MAG series. The 2026 MPG models are available in 1600W and 1300W variants and feature server-grade SiC MOSFETs designed to deliver a high level of stability and power efficiency. However, the standout addition is GPU Safeguard+, a new protection system aimed at preventing the overheating and melting issues associated with 12V-2x6 GPU power connectors.
